skill.name: pdf
skill.description: PDF processing skill for extracting text and tables, creating and modifying PDFs, merging or splitting pages, converting pages to images, and filling PDF forms.
skill.summary: Process, analyze, create, merge, split, and fill PDF documents with guided Python and command-line workflows.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: AI review found no confirmed malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or dynamic code execution. Most high-severity static findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, PDF terminology, test fixtures, and documentation URLs. The skill still has medium risk because helper scripts read and write user-specified local PDF, JSON, and image paths and the documentation instructs users to run external PDF tools.

medium_findings.title: Local File Read and Write Workflows
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The helper scripts intentionally read PDFs, JSON files, and images and write generated PDFs, JSON metadata, and PNG validation images. This is expected for a PDF automation skill, but it can overwrite user-selected output paths and processes untrusted document content through PDF libraries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are direct and semantically required by the skill. I found no evidence that they target sensitive paths or exfiltrate data, so the concern is controlled local-file impact rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: External PDF Tool Instructions
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The documentation instructs users to run command-line PDF utilities such as pdftotext, qpdf, pdftk, pdfimages, and pdftoppm. These are legitimate PDF workflows, but they execute local binaries against user-supplied documents and should be reviewed before use on untrusted files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and tied to PDF processing tasks. I did not find shell metacharacter construction or hidden command execution, so the risk is operational rather than command injection.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The detected URLs point to license terms, PDF specification documentation, a form-analysis article, and a pypdf issue reference. I found no code that sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in license text or comments. No HTTP client, fetch call, socket use, or network transmission code was found in the inspected files.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static detections for weak cryptography, C2 keywords, and system reconnaissance matched benign PDF-related words, sample JSON values, unit-test geometry data, and documentation text. No evidence found for malware control channels, cryptographic misuse, or host reconnaissance logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is visible as examples, test data, or normal PDF processing guidance. Targeted review found no malicious control flow, obfuscation, secret collection, or network destination.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Output Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts accept output paths from command-line arguments and write generated artifacts there. Users should choose dedicated working directories and avoid running the skill with elevated privileges.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The output writes are direct and intentional. The risk depends on user-selected paths, not on hidden behavior in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Processing Untrusted PDFs
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill opens PDFs with pypdf and pdf2image workflows. Malformed PDFs may trigger parser bugs or resource exhaustion in underlying libraries, so untrusted documents need sandboxed processing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The parsers are standard libraries used for legitimate PDF tasks. The risk is plausible for hostile PDFs but no exploit code or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: PDF work often requires many separate tools for extraction, conversion, merging, and form filling. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code guided workflows for common PDF processing tasks.
actual_capabilities: Extract text and page metadata from PDFs using pypdf, pdfplumber, and related tools.
Extract tables from PDF pages and export table data for spreadsheet workflows.
Create new PDFs with reportlab examples for text, pages, and simple report layouts.
Merge, split, rotate, crop, encrypt, decrypt, and watermark PDF files with Python or command-line tools.
Detect fillable PDF fields and generate JSON metadata for text fields, checkboxes, radio groups, and choices.
Fill non-fillable forms by converting pages to images, validating bounding boxes, and adding text annotations.
limitations: It depends on local Python libraries and optional PDF command-line tools that may not be installed.
OCR quality depends on image clarity, language support, and available Tesseract configuration.
Complex form layouts still need visual inspection and careful bounding box validation.
Password removal requires the correct document password and must follow user authorization rules.

use_cases.title: Prepare Legal Intake Forms
use_cases.description: Identify fillable fields, map client data to field identifiers, and produce a completed PDF for review.

use_cases.title: Extract Report Tables
use_cases.description: Pull tables and text from financial or research PDFs, then export structured data for analysis.

use_cases.title: Assemble Document Packets
use_cases.description: Merge, split, rotate, and watermark documents for internal packets or client-ready PDF bundles.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Text From a PDF
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for reading document content
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to inspect this file and extract its text. Summarize the page count, main sections, and any pages with sparse text.

prompt_templates.title: Merge and Organize PDFs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to merge these PDFs in the order I provide. Rotate pages that are sideways and create one final document with a clear filename.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a PDF Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to check whether this form has fillable fields. If it does, extract field metadata, map my answers to fields, and create a filled copy.

prompt_templates.title: Automate Batch PDF Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to design a repeatable workflow for extracting text and tables from all PDFs in this folder, with logs and separate output files.

output_examples.input: A user provides a scanned intake form and asks for a completed copy.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports that the form is not fillable, converts pages to images, identifies entry areas, validates bounding boxes, and creates a filled PDF with annotations.

output_examples.input: A user provides several quarterly report PDFs and asks for tables in spreadsheet-ready form.
output_examples.output: The assistant extracts detected tables page by page, notes pages with ambiguous layouts, and saves a combined spreadsheet for review.

output_examples.input: A user asks to merge three PDFs and rotate one page.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates a single merged PDF, applies the requested rotation, and confirms the page count and output file name.

best_practices: Work in a dedicated folder so generated PDFs, images, and JSON files do not overwrite important files.
Inspect generated validation images before filling non-fillable forms with annotations.
Run workflows on trusted PDFs or use sandboxing when documents come from unknown sources.
anti_patterns: Do not run password removal or decryption workflows without authorization from the document owner.
Do not skip bounding box validation when adding text annotations to non-fillable forms.
Do not assume OCR output is complete without checking low-quality scans and unusual layouts.

faq.question: Can this skill fill existing PDF form fields?
faq.answer: Yes. It can detect fillable fields, export field metadata, validate field values, and write a filled PDF.

faq.question: Can it fill PDFs that do not have form fields?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses page images, bounding boxes, validation images, and text annotations for non-fillable forms.

faq.question: Does it require extra software?
faq.answer: Some workflows require Python packages such as pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab, pdf2image, PIL, or optional command-line PDF tools.

faq.question: Can it extract tables from PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes pdfplumber examples for table extraction and spreadsheet export.

faq.question: Can it process scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, if OCR dependencies are installed. Results depend on scan quality and language support.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with sensitive documents?
faq.answer: It operates on local files in the provided scripts. Use a controlled workspace and review outputs before sharing them.
